mission.title: kepler: Cannot start up exporter with Kind
mission.description: Cannot start up exporter with Kind. Users encounter: "runtime error: index out of range [16] with length 16".

steps.title: Identify kepler troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kepler deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler -n kepler --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `runtime error: index out of range [16] with length 16`

steps.title: Review kepler configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kepler configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler
kubectl get configmap -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kepler
```
Try `kepler` with Kind, but the exporter cannot startup, get below errors in logs:
not sure if the Kind is not in the support list.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Cannot start up exporter with Kind
steps.description:
I checked the code again, I think the VM has the MSR support at least, since I can find `/dev/cpu/%d/msr`.
and for the problem of `physical_package_id`, here is the explained from official：
```
physical package id of cpuX. Typically corresponds to a physical socket number, 
but the actual value is
```yaml
[root@experimental kepler]# k -n kepler logs -f kepler-exporter-kgvdl
panic: runtime error: index out of range [16] with length 16

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/pkg/power/rapl/source.mapPackageAndCore()
	/opt/app-root/src/github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/pkg/power/rapl/source/msr_util.go:88 +0x234
github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/pkg/power/rapl/source.InitUnits()
	/opt/app-root/src/github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/pkg/power/rapl/source/msr_util.go:147 +0x1d
github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/pkg/power/rapl/source.(*Powe
```

steps.title: Confirm Cannot start up exporter with Kind is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler -n kepler --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kepler --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `runtime error: index out of range [16] with length 16` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
I checked the code again, I think the VM has the MSR support at least, since I can find `/dev/cpu/%d/msr`.
and for the problem of `physical_package_id`, here is the explained from official：
```
physical package id of cpuX. Typically corresponds to a physical socket number, 
but the actual value is architecture and platform dependent.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kepler installed or the issue environment reproducible.
